steps.title: Spot the positive signal
steps.content: The opening statement says the reception “has been very good,” which tells us the book was received favorably.

steps.title: Match the verb to that positive judgment
steps.content:
The blank needs a verb meaning to endorse or praise the book — context: a favorable reception, an award. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — attacked: criticized harshly; opposite of favorable reception. ✗
  • Choice B — recommended: endorsed or suggested favorably to others — fits the positive reception that led to award recognition. ✓
  • Choice C — overlooked: failed to notice; but reviewers, booksellers, and librarians clearly noticed (they generated the favorable reception). ✗
  • Choice D — misunderstood: failed to grasp the meaning; “misunderstood” conflicts with the favorable reception and award context. ✗
That matches Choice B — recommended.
